column1: What is the primary objective of the Lead-to-Cash process for Service?
column2: To provide an integrated SAP solution for running service processes, including master data and organizational integration.

column1: Which legacy SAP module did SAP S/4HANA Service replace (though still available in compatibility mode)?
column2: SAP Customer Service (CS).

column1: How does SAP S/4HANA Service eliminate functional redundancies between CRM and ERP?
column2: By unifying objects so they share the same database representation without needing middleware.

column1: Where is the SAP S/4HANA Service organizational structure integrated?
column2: It is fully integrated into the enterprise structure of SAP S/4HANA (specifically SD).

column1: In SAP S/4HANA Service, what tool is used to map task-related, functional structures like positions and employees?
column2: The Organizational Model.

column1: Which three central roles define a Business Partner in SAP Service?
column2: Account, Contact, and Employee.

column1: In the SAP Business Partner model, what is a "Contact"?
column2: A person with whom you have a business relationship, usually assigned to a corporate account.

column1: How is an internal "Employee" linked to the SAP system for service document defaults?
column2: The personnel master is linked to the Business Partner role "Employee" and the SAP User.

column1: Which three categories are used to differentiate "Products" in the material master for Service?
column2: Service Material, Component Planning (Spare Parts), and Structuring of Technical Objects.

column1: What specific field in the Product Master triggers the usage of a product in service documents?
column2: Item Category Group (e.g., Service Product or NORM).

column1: What four elements typically make up "Conditions" master data in Service?
column2: Prices, Surcharges/Discounts, Freight, and Taxes.

column1: Which technical object category represents a system area where objects can be installed?
column2: Functional Location.

column1: Which technical object category represents an individual object treated as an autonomous unit?
column2: Equipment.

column1: What is the purpose of a "Serial Number" in technical objects?
column2: To differentiate specific materials from others to treat them as individual items.

column1: What is a Service Contract?
column2: An outline agreement defining the content, scope, and price of services for a specific period.

column1: What are "Service Levels" in the context of a service contract?
column2: The specific entitlements or standards of service a customer is guaranteed.

column1: What must be created before a final Billing Document (Invoice) is generated for a service contract?
column2: A Billing Document Request.

column1: Define "Time-based" recurring service.
column2: Service tasks triggered after a specific time period has elapsed (e.g., every 6 months).

column1: Define "Performance-based" recurring service.
column2: Service tasks triggered when a specific counter reading is reached (e.g., every 10,000 km).

column1: Define "Condition-based" preventive maintenance.
column2: Maintenance triggered when a value (like temperature) falls outside a specified range.

column1: What is the function of a Service Order Template?
column2: To standardize recurring activities by defining services and spare parts in advance.

column1: What three components are linked to a "Maintenance Item" within a Maintenance Plan?
column2: Reference Object, Service Contract/Item, and Service Order Template.

column1: In maintenance scheduling, what does the "Cycle Start" date represent?
column2: The date from which the calculation of planned service dates begins.

column1: In maintenance scheduling, why is a "Call Date" usually set earlier than the "Planned Date"?
column2: To allow for a preprocessing phase, such as material procurement.

column1: Which maintenance scheduling function is used to trigger the next service order after the previous one is finished?
column2: Schedule.

column1: What is the primary purpose of the "Service Management Overview" app in Fiori?
column2: To provide a single screen for service managers to monitor key figures like expiring contracts and overdue orders.

column1: How does SAP S/4HANA Service benefit "Recurring Service" processes?
column2: By automatically generating service orders through the scheduling of maintenance plans.

column1: Which two specific service-related KPIs can a manager monitor in the "Service Management Overview" Fiori app?
column2: Expiring service contracts and incomplete service orders.

column1: What two items must be assigned to a "Maintenance Plan Item" to define the work and its entitlement?
column2: A Service Order Template and a Service Contract Item.

column1: What are the two primary cloud-based components of the SAP Service solution landscape?
column2: SAP Service Cloud and SAP Field Service Management.

column1: In the SAP system, which three entities can be linked to a personnel number for an internal Business Partner?
column2: The Position (org unit), the SAP User, and the Business Partner (Employee).

column1: Why is the "Sold-to Party" assigned at the Service Contract header (Details) level rather than the Item level?
column2: Because the Sold-to Party represents the primary business relationship for the entire contract, while items focus on specific products or technical objects.

column1: What is the mandatory sequence of documents required to charge a customer for a Service Contract?
column2: Billing Document Request followed by the Billing Document (Invoice).

column1: How can a "Serial Number" be used in relation to "Equipment" in technical object management?
column2: A serial number can be assigned to a piece of equipment to track it as an individual item.

column1: What is the function of an "Assembly" within the hierarchy of technical objects?
column2: To finely structure functional locations and equipment into non-individual Bill of Material (BOM) items.

column1: Which two core scope items represent the middle and later stages of the Lead-to-Cash Service process?
column2: Service Contract and Invoice Management, and Recurring Service.

column1: In an Organizational Model, what typically sits directly below the "Service Organization" node?
column2: The Service Back Office and Service Technicians.
